steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Cross-Text Connections question: use only what Text 1 says about Lorde's view to evaluate which answer she would use to push back against Text 2's argument. Text 2 argues that full-time workers lack the time needed to develop as poets. Lorde's rebuttal must come from her stated position in Text 1.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — By suggesting that those poets who publish in major poetry journals are most likely to be able to earn a living by writing poetry: Lorde says nothing about publishing in major journals or earning a living from poetry. Off-topic. ✗
  • Choice B — By pointing out that people can gain experience writing poetry while also working full-time jobs: Lorde explicitly says poets can write “between shifts, in the hospital pantry, on the subway, and on scraps of surplus paper” — a direct claim that full-time work does not block poetry practice. This is the natural counter to Text 2. ✓
  • Choice C — By mentioning that people often work full-time jobs in order to afford professional training in writing poetry: Lorde does not mention funding or professional training at all. ✗
  • Choice D — By indicating that those artists who specialize in more financially rewarding artistic forms are unlikely to also be successful as poets: Lorde makes no comparison between art forms in terms of financial reward or success rates. ✗
That is the correct answer.
